title: RESTRUCTURE: Use AVIF Files Directly - No Path Changes Needed for Production
problem: PNG conversion introduced blocky edges. AVIF files worked fine on public site. PNG maintenance is burden.
solution: Create self-contained project folder structure using AVIF files. Paths will be identical in dev and production.
applicable_rules: RULE_6: KNOWN_WORKING_CODE_ONLY - AVIF format proven working on production
RULE_2: NO_PARTIAL_COMPLIANCE - Complete restructure: organize → update paths → test → commit → push → verify
RULE_3: NO_SILENT_REGRESSION - Keep all 190 arrangements functional, no feature loss
RULE_7: EVIDENCE_BEFORE_ASSERTION - Test with curl before committing
RULE_16: COMPLETE_WORKFLOW_TESTING - Local test with AVIF files, verify smooth edges

current_broken_state.problem: Paths reference balloon-thumbnails/ at root level. Different in dev vs production.

subdirs.assets/: All image files (AVIF only, no PNGs)
subdirs.js/: JavaScript files (updated for new image paths)

new_self_contained_structure.benefit: Portable - copy entire balloon-planner/ folder to production, no path changes needed

steps.verbose_code_comment: Create nested asset folder structure: balloon-planner/assets/images/. This will hold ALL 190 AVIF files. Keeping everything under balloon-planner/ ensures portability - when deployed to public site, the entire folder moves together with correct relative paths.

steps.task_name: COPY AVIF files to new location
steps.action: Copy all 190 AVIF files from balloon-thumbnails/ to balloon-planner/assets/images/
steps.verbose_code_comment: Copy all AVIF files. These are proven working - they rendered smoothly on public site. AVIF format: (1) modern browser support (all current browsers), (2) no alpha fringing artifacts, (3) efficient file sizes, (4) scalable. Do NOT convert to PNG - that introduced the blocky edges problem.

steps.action: Change all image URLs from balloon-thumbnails/ to ./assets/images/
steps.input: gallery-data.js with 190 image URLs pointing to balloon-thumbnails/
steps.output: gallery-data.js with 190 image URLs pointing to ./assets/images/
steps.verbose_code_comment: Update paths: OLD: '../balloon-thumbnails/png-transparent/filename.png' → NEW: './assets/images/filename.avif'. This is a find-replace operation. Path is relative to balloon-planner/js/gallery-data.js, so ./assets/images/ resolves correctly. No domain-specific paths - works in dev and production identically.

steps.task_name: REGENERATE gallery-data.js from source AVIF files
steps.action: Run generate-gallery-data.js script, but read from balloon-planner/assets/images/ instead of balloon-thumbnails/
steps.input: Updated script pointing to new AVIF folder
steps.output: gallery-data.js with correct relative paths to AVIF files
steps.verbose_code_comment: Modify generate-gallery-data.js to: (1) read from balloon-planner/assets/images/*.avif, (2) output paths as './assets/images/filename.avif', (3) verify 190 AVIF files found, (4) verify 7 color families created. This ensures gallery-data.js is programmatically generated with correct paths.

steps.action: Use curl to check: (1) HTML loads, (2) CSS loads, (3) gallery-data.js loads, (4) paths in gallery-data.js are correct, (5) AVIF files accessible
steps.output: Curl verification showing all resources HTTP 200, paths correct, AVIF files found
steps.verbose_code_comment: Verification steps: (1) curl http://localhost:8888/balloon-planner/index-photo.html → HTTP 200, (2) grep gallery-data.js for './assets/images/' → all 190 paths use new location, (3) curl to verify AVIF files accessible at new paths, (4) check no 404 errors in server logs.

steps.task_name: VISUAL TEST: Verify smooth AVIF edges in browser
steps.action: Open page in browser, look at color swatches - should be smooth with NO blocky edges
steps.output: Visual confirmation: swatches are smooth, clear, professional appearance (AVIF edges are clean)
steps.verbose_code_comment: Expected result: (1) Color swatch thumbnails display smoothly, (2) No blocky edges visible (AVIF format has no alpha fringing artifacts), (3) Images are sharp and professional, (4) Gallery filters work (click color → gallery updates), (5) No console errors in F12 DevTools.

steps.action: Test complete app: 190 arrangements load, color filtering works, reset button works, responsive layout works, no console errors
steps.input: Updated app with new folder structure
steps.output: Confirmation: all functionality works, no regression
steps.verbose_code_comment: Test suite: (1) Page loads without errors, (2) All 7 color families visible as swatches, (3) Click each color → gallery filters to that color's arrangements, (4) Click Reset → gallery shows all 190 again, (5) Click arrangement thumbnail → main image updates, (6) Click main image → lightbox opens, (7) Responsive on mobile (browser resize to 375px width).

steps.action: Delete: png-transparent/ folder (if exists), generate-gallery-data.js (if PNG-specific), fix-avif-edges.sh (no longer needed)
steps.input: Old files from PNG conversion attempt
steps.output: Project cleaned up, only AVIF files remain
steps.verbose_code_comment: Cleanup ensures: (1) No confusion between PNG and AVIF versions, (2) No accidental use of old paths, (3) Cleaner git history, (4) Smaller final package size. Keep only: AVIF files in assets/images/, updated gallery-data.js, original scripts.

steps.task_name: GIT COMMIT restructure and AVIF migration
steps.action: Stage all changes: new folder structure, updated gallery-data.js, deleted PNG files
steps.output: Git commit with message describing migration
steps.verbose_code_comment: Commit message: 'refactor: Restructure to use AVIF files directly - eliminate PNG conversion artifacts. Move all images to self-contained assets/images/ folder. Update gallery-data.js paths for portability. Dev and production paths now identical. All 190 arrangements use AVIF format for smooth, professional edges.'

steps.action: git push origin main (or default branch)
steps.verbose_code_comment: Push restructured code to GitHub. If auto-deploy configured, production will receive: (1) all 190 AVIF files in assets/images/, (2) updated gallery-data.js with correct relative paths, (3) cleaned up project structure. No path changes needed - dev and production are identical.

steps.task_name: VERIFY production: Test on deployed site
steps.action: Access deployed balloon planner, verify smooth AVIF edges, all 190 arrangements visible, filtering works
steps.output: Confirmation: production app working with smooth edges, no artifacts
steps.verbose_code_comment: Production verification: (1) AVIF files load from production assets/images/ folder, (2) Swatch thumbnails display smoothly (AVIF format, no artifacts), (3) Gallery functions correctly (190 arrangements, 7 color families), (4) Responsive on mobile, (5) No console errors. Success = smooth edges return, proven format, portable structure.

technical: AVIF format proven working on production
No conversion artifacts (PNG conversion caused blocky edges)
Self-contained folder structure - copy entire balloon-planner/ to any server
Relative paths work identically in dev and production
No maintenance burden - no complex scripts needed
operational: One image format (AVIF) - simpler to manage
All 190 files in one folder structure
scalability: If adding more arrangements - just add AVIF files to assets/images/
If moving to new server - entire balloon-planner/ folder is portable
If updating images - regenerate AVIF from sources, same folder structure

must_have: All 190 AVIF files copied to balloon-planner/assets/images/
Page loads without 404 errors (all AVIF files accessible)
Swatch thumbnails display smoothly (no blocky edges)
Reset button returns to viewing all 190 arrangements
No console errors in browser DevTools
Local test passes with curl verification
